mentions.note: At 42x earnings, valuation too rich despite AI leadership; software sector under pressure from AI competition. Stock failed to bounce on Friday weakness. Wait for better entry.
Down $100 to one-year lows. Enterprise software chart (Adobe, Salesforce also struggling) awful; too hard to call bottom. Cramer won't catch falling knife.
Alongside Salesforce, SaaS leaders; both doing well but stocks not playing ball. NOW down 100+ pts from high. Bill McDermott must explain if broken stock vs broken company.

Stock down 45% from peak but Q4 beat; $5B buyback ($2B accelerated) signals management conviction. AI not eroding software; consolidating feature companies. Rule of 57 metrics impressive; partnership with Anthropic validates platform approach.
Multiple compression, not earnings failure; down 50% YoY, P/E collapsed 65× to 28×; market fears AI will reduce per-user value; wait for multiple bottom before buying despite excellent business.

#4 worst January, down ~24%, despite beat on all metrics with excellent guidance (fell 10% on earnings anyway). P/E crushed 64x → 28x forward. CEO Bill McDermott strong but sentiment broken; wait for stabilization.
Down 7% in software rotation; reported good numbers but stock withering; enterprise workflow software facing AI displacement skepticism.
Down 50% from high, 24x P/E (down from 70x end-2024); 19% earnings growth; $2B accelerated buyback announced; starting to look good.
Paper specifically targeted ServiceNow on concern clients will shift to AI-powered in-house development; multiple at risk.
Huge rally in line with other enterprise software cohort due to program buying. Has been a horrendous real dog, but today's spike is mechanical, not fundamental.
Up 3.5% on rally; AI displacement fears overstated; strong fundamentals support valuation.
Enterprise software bounce overdue; stocks beaten down as if going out of business despite CEOs pivoting to combat AI competition.
Trading at 26x earnings (cheap for growth stock) but SaaS sector intensely disliked on Wall Street. Despite respecting CEO Bill McDermott, expect more turbulence ahead.
Enterprise workflow software facing disruption from AI upstarts; lock-in advantage eroding as natural-language access democratizes software.
Enterprise software hammered by hardware rotation; down 8%.
Expanded buyback and CEO buying at higher prices; chronic underperformer down 42% YTD; watch for analyst upgrades on rebound.
Heavy selling from shareholders on Anthropic disruption fears (unfounded); Bill McDermott expected solid quarter but may be ignored again; Cramer avoiding hornet's nest despite liking company.

SaaS workflow automation platform; beat-and-raise Q1 (RPO +23.5% to $28B, AI revenue guidance raised to $1.5B). Operating margin 31.5% (down 50bps on acquisitions, efficiency recovering). CEO McDermott argues rule of 60 emerging; stock hammered despite strong fundamentals—Cramer suggests traditional metrics becoming less relevant but cheap valuations could reverse sentiment.

Down 44% YTD, 17% today despite beating Rule of 50 and strong AI adoption; market refusing premium multiple due to AI pricing pressure risk. Stock valuation still expensive at 37x earnings even after crash.
SaaS hammered by AI displacement worries; analyst meeting Monday will reveal if customers cutting contract length; software group caught fire today though.
Part of software rotation; up 5% today. Cramer questions if move can be maintained given AI displacement concerns.
Stunning strength as software sector recovers from recent weakness; significant run suggests end-of-May rotation favoring previously beaten-down names.
Expensive SaaS with rapid pivot underway; better than most enterprise software peers but not the time to buy. Sometimes bullishness has to wait.
